title: Shared documentation
description: Write SKILL.md at .apm/skills/pyxlsx/SKILL.md. Content must cover: quick command reference with example invocations and JSON output for all 11 commands, the output contract (JSON default, --plain, errors to stderr, exit codes), the formula evaluation caveat (open in Excel/LibreOffice before relying on cached values), ready-made openpyxl script patterns for table creation and bulk writes, and a cross-skill agent workflow example (extract from xlsx with table read, process, write to pptx via pypptx).
acceptance_criteria: All 12 commands are documented with example invocations and representative JSON output
Output contract section explains JSON default, --plain, stderr, and exit codes
openpyxl escape hatch section provides working script patterns for table creation and cell writes
Formula evaluation caveat is clearly explained
Agent workflow section shows an extract-xlsx-to-pptx example

handoff_summary.completed: SKILL.md created at .apm/skills/pyxlsx/SKILL.md covering: quick command reference for all 11 commands with example invocations and representative JSON output; output contract (JSON default, --plain, stderr errors, exit codes); formula evaluation caveat (data_only=True reads cached values; open in Excel/LibreOffice first if you need evaluated results); openpyxl escape hatch with table-creation and bulk-write script patterns; cross-skill agent workflow showing extract from xlsx with table read, process, write to pptx via pypptx.
handoff_summary.remaining: None. This bead's only deliverable was the SKILL.md file.
handoff_summary.risks: Acceptance criteria say "All 12 commands" but only 11 are implemented in cli.py. The bead description itself says "all 11 commands". Documentation matches the actual implementation (11 commands). No action needed unless a 12th command is added later.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflict risks. SKILL.md is a new file not touched by any sibling bead.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Documented data_only=True behavior accurately from the actual inspect.py code (not the spec's description of data_only=False). The spec description was incorrect — the implementation uses data_only=True for reads. Formula caveat section explains this correctly. All JSON examples taken from the spec where they matched the implementation signatures verified in cli.py, inspect.py, write.py, and pack.py.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: No tests added or modified. Documentation only.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: Acceptance criteria count (12) does not match implemented command count (11). Documented all 11 that exist.

conflict_risks: No conflict risks. SKILL.md is a new file not touched by any sibling bead.

execution_history.summary: Created .apm/skills/pyxlsx/SKILL.md with all 11 commands documented, output contract, formula evaluation caveat, openpyxl escape hatch patterns, and extract-xlsx-to-pptx agent workflow example.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.

worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks:
pyproject.toml declares packages = [".apm/skills/pyxlsx/pyxlsx"]. Any bead that modifies pyproject.toml must preserve this path. No other files in this bead conflict with sibling beads.
Sibling beads that also modify pyproject.toml dev deps may conflict on that block. The conftest.py change is additive and should not conflict with existing pptx fixtures.
No conflicts expected. This file was new; no other bead claims cli.py.
cli.py has two downstream beads adding more commands (sheet read, table read, cell get/set, etc.). Conflicts likely at the end of the file where new command groups/commands are appended. Keep the existing group stubs (sheet, table, cell) in place.
No sibling beads touch these files. Clean merge expected.
No sibling beads overlap inspect.py or cli.py per the dependency graph; no conflict risk identified.
No conflicts expected. write.py is a new file with no overlap. cli.py additions are append-only to the cell group with no edits to existing commands.
No overlapping file edits with sibling beads expected; write.py and cli.py are owned solely by this bead within the feature tree.
No conflicts expected. pack.py is a new file. cli.py edits are append-only (new import + two new commands at the end of file) so they will merge cleanly with any sibling bead changes to the top of the file.
